ScenarioName: Spear Phishing to Finance Team
Description: Attackers send realistic emails to finance staff using known supplier names.

ScenarioName: Lookalike Domain Phishing
Description: Emails come from a domain visually similar to the organisation's official one.

ScenarioName: Link to Convincing Fake Login Page
Description: A realistic fake portal captures staff credentials when they sign in.

ScenarioName: Multiple Unreported Phishing Attempts
Description: Several staff receive phishing messages but none are reported to IT.

ScenarioName: Phishing Bypass of Simple Filters
Description: Attackers slightly alter text and images to bypass basic email filters.

ScenarioName: Credential Theft Leads to Mailbox Rule Abuse
Description: Compromised mailbox is used to auto-forward or hide important emails.

ScenarioName: Phishing Used to Access Cloud Admin Portal
Description: Stolen credentials are used to sign in to a cloud admin portal without MFA.

ScenarioName: Common Password Reuse Across Systems
Description: Users reuse the same password for email, VPN and third-party services.

ScenarioName: No Regular Access Review
Description: Access rights are only adjusted when staff complain, never proactively reviewed.

ScenarioName: Admin Activities from Unhardened Laptops
Description: Administrators perform changes from standard user laptops used for email and web browsing.

ScenarioName: Generic Shared Admin Accounts
Description: Critical systems are administered using shared 'admin' accounts.

ScenarioName: Cloud Accounts Without Conditional Access
Description: Cloud accounts used by staff have no conditional access or geo restrictions.

ScenarioName: Dormant Admin Accounts Not Removed
Description: Old admin accounts remain enabled long after staff have moved role or left.

ScenarioName: Self-Registered Third-Party Accounts Using Work Email
Description: Staff use work email to sign up to unsanctioned cloud services.

ScenarioName: No MFA for High-Risk Cloud Apps
Description: Key SaaS tools (HR, finance) are accessible from the internet with only passwords.

ScenarioName: Unclear Data Ownership
Description: No named owners for critical datasets used by essential functions.

ScenarioName: Large Sensitive Dataset in Personal OneDrive
Description: A large export of personal data is stored in an individual's cloud storage.

ScenarioName: Unencrypted Backups Taken to Offsite on USB
Description: Backups are periodically carried offsite on unencrypted USB drives.

ScenarioName: Inadequate Retention Controls
Description: Historic data is kept for many years without business or legal justification.

ScenarioName: Misconfigured Cloud Bucket Exposes Non-Critical Data
Description: A cloud storage bucket containing internal but non-sensitive documents is publicly reachable.

ScenarioName: Infrequently Tested Backups
Description: Backups are taken but restoration tests rarely occur.

ScenarioName: Highly Sensitive Data on Shared Network Drive
Description: A shared drive holds highly sensitive data with broad read access.

ScenarioName: No Clear Process for Data Breach Handling
Description: Organisation lacks a defined approach to respond to potential data breaches.

ScenarioName: Internet-Facing Service with Weak TLS
Description: A web service supporting staff uses outdated TLS configuration.

ScenarioName: Flat Network with Exposed Management Interfaces
Description: Management interfaces for infrastructure are reachable from general user subnets.

ScenarioName: Patch Delays on Internet-Facing Systems
Description: External services are patched only a few times per year.

ScenarioName: Unsupported OS on Internal File Server
Description: An internal file server runs an OS version past end-of-support.

ScenarioName: Weak Wi-Fi Controls for Staff Network
Description: Staff Wi-Fi uses a pre-shared key rather than individual credentials.

ScenarioName: No Central Logging for Firewall or VPN
Description: Key network devices do not send logs to a central collector.

ScenarioName: Malware Outbreak on Several PCs
Description: Multiple endpoints show activity consistent with a malware outbreak.

ScenarioName: Critical External Service Without DDoS Consideration
Description: A public-facing service has no specific capacity or DDoS resilience planning.

ScenarioName: Remote Admin Interface Exposed from Internet
Description: A management interface for a core system is reachable directly from the internet.

ScenarioName: Local Admin Rights for Most Staff
Description: Many staff devices run with local administrator privileges.

ScenarioName: Shadow IT Apps Installed by Users
Description: Users install unapproved software on their work devices.

ScenarioName: No Regular Review of Security Logs
Description: Security logs are collected but not routinely reviewed.

ScenarioName: Third-Party Support Access Not Monitored
Description: External suppliers connect for support without detailed monitoring.

ScenarioName: Outdated Anti-Malware Signatures
Description: Endpoint protection signatures have not been updated for weeks.

ScenarioName: Poor Documentation of Network Topology
Description: Network diagrams are outdated, hindering incident response.

ScenarioName: No Tested Disaster Recovery for Key System
Description: An essential system has never had its disaster recovery plan tested.
